stemHtml: CS-23 and CS-25 specify the requirements for an aircraft's design manoeuvring speed (VA). Given that VA is an equivalent airspeed (EAS), it should remain relevant at most altitudes. However, for jet aeroplanes with conventional flight controls, its value may become unreliable at high altitude because the…

options.a: airspeed indication is based on a Mach number at high altitude, making VA as an EAS irrelevant.
options.b: higher stall speed and low control forces make it easier to produce a high load factor.
options.c: airspeed indication is invalid due to the compressibility effects at high subsonic Mach numbers.
options.d: the wings may enter a shock stall if flying at the low-altitude VA since the aeroplane will exceed MCRIT.
